prd.oneLiner: Connect products, prices, transactions, payments, returns, inventory, settlement, and store performance in one POS flow.
prd.goal: Improve the accuracy of store sales and settlement data and reduce time spent on return and discount exceptions.
prd.whyNow: When sales, payments, discounts, returns, inventory, and settlement are disconnected, teams cannot quickly identify store status or the cause of revenue variances.
prd.problem: When sales, payments, discounts, returns, inventory, and settlement are disconnected, teams cannot quickly identify store status or the cause of revenue variances.
prd.solution: Connect products, prices, transactions, payments, returns, inventory, settlement, and store performance in one POS flow.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Store associate and manager handling sales, payments, and returns
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: When sales, payments, discounts, returns, inventory, and settlement are disconnected, teams cannot quickly identify store status or the cause of revenue variances.

targets.name: Retail operations and store-systems coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: When sales, payments, discounts, returns, inventory, and settlement are disconnected, teams cannot quickly identify store status or the cause of revenue variances.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: When sales, payments, discounts, returns, inventory, and settlement are disconnected, teams cannot quickly identify store status or the cause of revenue variances.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Improve the accuracy of store sales and settlement data and reduce time spent on return and discount exceptions.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Product, price, promotion, store, and payment-method registration
Sale, payment, return, inventory, employee, and store-performance lookup
Sale processing, discount application, payment, return, settlement, and exception approval
Revenue, basket size, product, payment, return, and store-performance reporting
Product, price, promotion, store, and payment-method registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
